steps.text: Output mappers automatically mask sensitive patterns in tool responses using regex. Example: API keys, IP addresses, email addresses.
Look at the tool response above - identify sensitive patterns you want to mask (API keys, tokens, IPs, emails, etc.).
Enter a regex pattern to match those sensitive values. Example: '\b[A-Z0-9]{32}\b' for 32-character API keys, or '\b\d{1,3}(\.\d{1,3}){3}\b' for IP addresses.
Set an alias prefix like 'api_key_' to generate readable aliases: 'api_key_1', 'api_key_2', etc.

Click 'Preview' to test your regex on the actual tool response above. You'll see exactly what gets masked, and a 'Save Mapper' button will appear next to it — click that to apply the rule to all future responses.
